senses.definition: People who have an irrational fear or strong dislike of cats.
examples: Because of the number of ailurophobes in the group, the host decided not to bring her cat into the room.
Support groups for ailurophobes can help them slowly feel safer around cats.

senses.definition: An extreme and irrational fear of cats.
examples: Her ailurophobia was so severe that she crossed the street to avoid walking past a cat.
A therapist diagnosed him with ailurophobia after he experienced panic attacks near cats.

senses.definition: Different instances or types of an extreme and irrational fear of cats.
examples: Clinicians may encounter various ailurophobias, from mild discomfort to intense panic around cats.

senses.definition: Having an extreme and irrational fear or strong dislike of cats.
examples: His ailurophobic reaction made it clear he could not stay in a house with cats.
The study focused on ailurophobic patients and their responses to therapy.

senses.definition: A genus of mammals that includes the giant panda.
examples: The giant panda belongs to the genus Ailuropoda.
Researchers compared Ailuropoda with other bear genera to study their evolution.

senses.definition: A genus of mammals that includes the panda-like species Ailuropus melanoleucus in some older classifications.
examples: In older taxonomies, the giant panda was once placed in the genus Ailuropus.
Comparisons between Ailuropus and Ailuropoda reflect changes in scientific classification.

senses.definition: A genus of mammals that includes the red panda.
examples: The red panda is the only living species in the genus Ailurus.
Scientists studied Ailurus to understand its diet and habitat.

senses.definition: A common name for certain plants, especially yarrow, once used in folk medicine.
examples: In some rural areas, people still refer to yarrow as ailweed.
The herbal guide listed ailweed as a remedy for minor pains.

senses.definition: To point a weapon, object, or action at a target.
examples: He aimed the camera at the mountains to take a picture.
The player carefully aimed the ball at the corner of the goal.

senses.definition: To try or plan to achieve something.
examples: We aim to finish the project by Friday.
The company aims to reduce its waste by half.

senses.definition: The purpose or result that someone wants to achieve.
examples: The main aim of the meeting is to share information.
Her aim is to become a doctor.

senses.definition: The act of directing something, especially a weapon or ball, toward a target.
examples: His aim was perfect, and he hit the center of the target.
Practicing every day improved her aim in basketball.

senses.definition: Capable of being aimed or directed at something.
examples: The device has an aimable nozzle for more precise spraying.
Engineers designed an aimable light to focus on specific areas of the stage.

senses.definition: An older spelling of 'aimag', meaning a major administrative division or tribe in Mongolia or Central Asia.
examples: The caravan crossed from one aimak into another.
Each aimak was ruled by its own local leader.

senses.definition: An older or variant spelling of 'Aymara', referring to an Indigenous people of the Andes and their language.
examples: The aimara are known for their rich traditions and textiles.
She studied aimara oral poetry at university.

senses.definition: Planned or tried to achieve something.
examples: The program aimed to help young entrepreneurs.
Their campaign was aimed at raising awareness about climate change.

senses.definition: Pointed a weapon, object, or action at a target.
examples: He aimed his arrow at the center of the target.
She aimed the hose at the flowers.

senses.definition: A female given name.
examples: Aimée invited all her friends to the party.
The book was dedicated to someone named Aimee.

senses.definition: A person or device that aims or directs something at a target.
examples: The gunner relied on an automatic aimer to adjust for distance.
As the team’s best aimer, she usually took the final shot.

senses.definition: People or devices that aim or direct something at a target.
examples: Modern tanks are equipped with advanced aimers.
Skilled aimers were selected for the archery competition.

senses.definition: Having a clear purpose or direction.
examples: She took aimful steps toward her long-term goals.
His career has been marked by aimful choices.

senses.definition: In a way that shows clear purpose or direction.
examples: She moved aimfully through each stage of the plan.
He spoke aimfully about the changes he wanted to make.

senses.definition: Pointing a weapon, object, or action at a target.
examples: She was aiming the ball at the top corner of the net.
The photographer was aiming the lens at the skyline.

senses.definition: Trying or planning to achieve something.
examples: They are aiming to open a new store next year.
The course is aiming at improving students’ writing skills.

senses.definition: Without a clear purpose, plan, or direction.
examples: He spent an aimless year traveling from city to city.
Their discussion became aimless and went on for hours.

senses.definition: In a way that has no clear purpose, plan, or direction.
examples: They walked aimlessly around the town for hours.
He flipped aimlessly through the channels on TV.

senses.definition: The state of having no clear purpose, plan, or direction.
examples: After losing his job, he struggled with a feeling of aimlessness.
The story’s slow pace and aimlessness bored some readers.

senses.definition: Different kinds or instances of having no clear purpose, plan, or direction.
examples: The novel explores various aimlessnesses of modern life.

senses.definition: A name used for certain Indigenous groups or languages in South America in older sources.
examples: The explorer reported meeting a group called the Aimore.
Some maps from the period still label the region as home to the Aimore.

senses.definition: Purposes or results that someone or something wants to achieve.
examples: The course has three main aims.
Their long-term aims include expanding into Asia.

senses.definition: Points something at a target or tries to achieve something.
examples: She always aims for the top corner of the net.
The policy aims to protect small businesses.

senses.definition: The quality of being worth aiming at or striving for.
examples: They discussed the aimworthiness of each possible project.
The book questions the aimworthiness of constant economic growth.

senses.definition: A dialect form of 'own', used especially in some Scottish and northern English speech.
examples: He went his ain way despite their advice.
She baked the bread with her ain hands.

senses.definition: A Yiddish expression meaning "it happened to him" or used to note something that occurred to someone, often with a resigned tone.
examples: When he slipped on the ice, someone muttered, "ainaleh."
She shrugged and said "ainaleh" when told about his misfortune.

senses.definition: In Irish mythology, a goddess associated with summer, love, and the sun; also used as a female given name.
examples: Aine was honored in myths as a goddess of the sun and fertility.
Her name is Aine, after the Irish goddess.

senses.definition: In some South Asian use, an elder sister.
examples: He always listened to his ainee’s advice.
Her ainee helped pay for her education.

senses.definition: A term used in some Pacific Island contexts for a family group or relatives.
examples: He returned home to visit his ainga.
The whole ainga gathered for the celebration.

senses.definition: Family groups or relatives in some Pacific Island contexts.
examples: Different aingas shared the land and its resources.
The event brought together several aingas from the region.

senses.definition: A rare disease in which a toe, usually the little toe, slowly becomes constricted and may eventually fall off.
examples: The doctor diagnosed the painful condition in his toe as ainhum.
Ainhum is most often reported in adults living in tropical regions.

senses.definition: A plural form of the Greek word "ainos", meaning praise or a saying, used in some scholarly or religious texts.
examples: The hymns were described as ainoi of gratitude and joy.
In the manuscript, several ainoi are directed to different saints.

senses.definition: A dialect or archaic form of 'ones', used in some Scottish and northern English varieties.
examples: He chose the ains he liked best.
These ains are mine, and those are yours.

senses.definition: A Scottish dialect form meaning 'myself' or 'oneself'.
examples: I’ll just do it ainsell.
He said he would manage it ainsell.

senses.definition: A Scottish dialect plural form meaning 'ourselves' or 'yourselves'.
examples: We’ll take care of it ainsells.
They said they could handle it ainsells.

senses.definition: A non-standard spelling of "ain't", used in informal speech to mean 'am not', 'is not', 'are not', 'has not', or 'have not'.
examples: I aint going if it rains.
She aint finished her homework yet.

senses.definition: An Indigenous people of northern Japan and nearby regions, or their language.
examples: The Ainu have fought to preserve their culture and language.
She took a course in Ainu history at university.

senses.definition: A plural form referring to members of the Ainu people.
examples: Many Ainus now live in urban areas.
The museum exhibit focuses on the lives of Ainus in the past.

senses.definition: A thick sauce made mainly of garlic and oil, often with egg yolk, served with meat, fish, or vegetables.
examples: The grilled fish was served with a rich garlic aioli.
She dipped her fries into a lemony aioli.

senses.definition: Different kinds or servings of garlic-based aioli sauce.
examples: The restaurant offers several aiolis, including chili and herb versions.
They ordered a platter of vegetables with two aiolis.

senses.definition: A term from Greek meaning an age, lifetime, or eternity, used in philosophy and theology.
examples: The philosopher wrote about the aion as an endless cycle of time.
In some texts, aion is translated as 'age' rather than 'eternity'.

senses.definition: Relating to an age or eternity, especially in older religious or philosophical texts.
examples: The writer uses 'aionial life' to mean life belonging to the coming age.
Scholars debate the precise meaning of aionial punishment.

senses.definition: The mixture of gases around the earth that people and animals breathe.
examples: The air felt fresh and cool after the storm.
Open the window to let some air into the room.

senses.definition: The space above the ground or around things.
examples: The bird suddenly jumped into the air.
Balloons filled the air during the festival.

senses.definition: A particular feeling or quality that something seems to have.
examples: There was an air of sadness in the room.
He spoke with an air of confidence.

senses.definition: To let fresh air into a place.
examples: She opened the windows to air the bedroom.
It's good to air the room every morning.

senses.definition: To express your opinions or complaints publicly.
examples: The workers aired their concerns at the meeting.
She used social media to air her complaints.

senses.definition: To broadcast something on radio or television.
examples: The new documentary will air next week.
The interview was aired on national television.

senses.definition: A genus of small grasses found in various regions, sometimes called hair-grass.
examples: The botanist identified several species of Aira in the meadow.
Aira often grows in poor, sandy soils.

senses.definition: Suitable to be broadcast on radio or television.
examples: The producer decided that the content was not airable before 9 p.m.
They worked to make the show more family-friendly and airable.

senses.definition: Able to be given fresh air or ventilated.
examples: The cushions are made with removable, airable covers.
Choose an airable storage space to prevent mold.

senses.definition: A cactus from South America whose seeds are used as a natural red or purple dye.
examples: Traditional weavers used airampo to dye their wool.
The seeds of airampo produce a deep red color.

senses.definition: A cold drink made from yogurt mixed with water and salt, common in Middle Eastern and Central Asian countries.
examples: We ordered airan to drink with our spicy kebabs.
A glass of chilled airan is very refreshing in hot weather.

senses.definition: A safety device in a vehicle that quickly fills with gas in a crash to protect the people inside.
examples: The airbag inflated instantly when the car hit the tree.
Newer cars have multiple airbags to protect drivers and passengers.
